chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Civil Revision Application No.153 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For hearing of C.M.A No.2566/2025 2. For hearing of main case 02.01.2026 Mr.Rakesh Kumar, Advocate holding brief for Mr.Ashar Majeed Khokhar, Advocate for applicant ********** This matter was put before me on 24.12.2025, when I had queried the Counsel regarding the maintainability of the revision and matter was adjourned for today i.e 02.01.2026. The applicant/defendant is aggrieved by the order dated 04.11.2025 passed in Summary Suit No. 64/2025 by the learned VI‐Additional District Judge, Hyderabad, whereby the leave to defend application was granted conditionally. The applicant/defendant states that no ground was made out as per the leave to defend application for the grant of conditional leave and that such leave ought to have been allowed unconditionally. The criteria for granting conditional or unconditional leave to defend lies within the discretion of the trial Court. The learned VI‐Additional District Judge, Hyderabad, has articulated his reasons in the impugned order for exercising such discretion. I have perused the same and do not find any illegality or irregularity warranting interference. Accordingly, the revision application is dismissed in the above terms. JUDGE AHSAN K. ABRO
